channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18693] sanity-lnet: nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Sergey Cheremencev <scherementsev@ddn.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ad35ccfd-9d51-4822-983f-49b83d52bd79

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/110784 - 5.15.0-94-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/110784 - 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Session failed with status ABORT. sanity-lnet completed successfully. After completion there is a lot of messages like:

[21792.120241] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying
[21822.838662] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out
[22003.436593] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out
...
[27155.664682] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out

From the MDS:

[25994.958602] Autotest: Test running for 430 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_110784.43)
[26298.043672] Autotest: Test running for 435 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_110784.43)
[26402.490385] LustreError: lustre-OST0006-osc-MDT0000: operation ost_connect to node 10.240.22.243@tcp failed: rc = -19
[26402.497237] LustreError: Skipped 3 previous similar messages
[26601.219038] Autotest: Test running for 440 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_110784.43)
[26904.277762] Autotest: Test running for 445 minutes (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_110784.43)
[26965.747102] Autotest: Timing session out! Timeout has been exceeded but the test framework is still running (lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_110784.43)
item.summary: sanity-lnet: nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Thanks Charlie, I think this will resolve quite a number of spurious test failures that in turn put extra load on the build and test nodes.

I investigated our NFS servers and discovered the ARP table limits had the default GC values which are far too low for the amount of traffic it handles:

Default limit: 1,024 ARP entries
Actual connections: 1,391 (onyx-204), 1,436 (trevis-204)

Impact

  • ARP table at 100% capacity (1,022 / 1,024 entries)
  • Kernel performing 2,788 forced garbage collections per second
  • Hundreds of NFSv4 "testing state ID with incorrect client ID" errors per second
  • Connection instability and performance degradation

I increased the limits yesterday and the ARP entries balooned up to 1,700, all of the GCs stopped and the "testing state ID with incorrect client ID" errors went away. This was likely the cause of this ticket. I'm closing it. Please re-open with any new occurrences.

+1 with sanity-sec: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5185d3bf-6584-4b49-a4b6-a1ae97812075

[21588.004720] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity-sec test 72d: fileset inheritance for dynamic nodemap ========================================================== 15:51:56 (1758642716)
[21628.032870] Lustre: 1127748:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1758642741/real 0]  req@ffff8d5145e4f0c0 x1844069543450880/t0(0) o400->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff8d5161535800@10.240.29.19@tcp:12/10 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758642757 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[21628.042301] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff8d5161535800: Connection to lustre-MDT0000 (at 10.240.29.19@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will wait for recovery to complete
[21628.045460] LustreError: MGC10.240.29.19@tcp: Connection to MGS (at 10.240.29.19@tcp) was lost; in progress operations using this service will fail
[21633.152839] Lustre: 1127746:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1758642746/real 0]  req@ffff8d51553e23c0 x1844069543451136/t0(0) o400->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff8d5161535800@10.240.29.19@tcp:12/10 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758642762 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[21633.158909] Lustre: 1127746:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 1 previous similar message
[21638.272801] Lustre: 1127745:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1758642751/real 0]  req@ffff8d51553e3a80 x1844069543451392/t0(0) o400->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff8d5161535800@10.240.29.19@tcp:12/10 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758642767 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[21642.368741] Lustre: 1127747:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has timed out for sent delay: [sent 1758642756/real 0]  req@ffff8d5145e4d6c0 x1844069543451648/t0(0) o400->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff8d5161535800@10.240.29.19@tcp:12/10 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758642772 ref 2 fl Rpc:XNr/200/ffffffff rc 0/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[21802.367410] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying
[21817.727254] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out
[21817.727253] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying
[21823.871225] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.29.19@tcp, 10.240.29.13@tcp
[21823.871301] Lustre: 1127746:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1758642746/real 1758642953]  req@ffff8d51553e30c0 x1844069543451008/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.29.19@tcp@10.240.29.19@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758642762 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
[21823.878609] Lustre: 1127746:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 1 previous similar message
[21823.880525] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.29.19@tcp at host 10.240.29.19:7988
[21824.895290] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.29.13@tcp at host 10.240.29.13:7988

+1 failure with sanity-dom: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/d6ca2ffb-9b6c-4f20-96a4-0a41d9f35fdd

[21849.913639] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.46.115@tcp at host 10.240.46.115:7988
[21914.425398] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.46.115@tcp at host 10.240.46.115:7988
[21914.426967] LNetError: Skipped 2 previous similar messages
[21966.649256] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.46.115@tcp, 10.240.46.114@tcp
[21977.273242] nfs: server 10.240.32.204 not responding, timed out
[21982.265211] nfs: server 10.240.32.204 not responding, timed out

+1 on Master https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/52508c8a-a008-4899-96b2-93d464b03230

Failed TC: sanity-sec/75a
Regression: No / Not determine
Reproducible: No (Mostly passes on rerun)

Client Kernel version    5.15.0-142-generic
Server Kernel version    4.18.0-553.71.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

client log:

...
[21803.375323] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21803.375380] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, timed out^M            
[21812.143052] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.24.50@tcp, 10.240.24.53@tcp^M
[21812.144817] Lustre: 1121431:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1758154259/real 1758154452]  req@ffff8b5909aa2700 x      1843557076257920/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.24.53@tcp@10.240.24.53@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1758154275 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid      :4294967295^M
[21812.150827] Lustre: 1121431:0:(client.c:2458:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) Skipped 11 previous similar messages^M
[21812.151443] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.24.53@tcp at host 10.240.24.53:7988^M
[21813.167011] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.24.50@tcp at host 10.240.24.50:7988^M
...

on master:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/a4a89b54-124a-4670-80f4-4457b9e19174

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/fac37689-e2ea-4117-8211-a2fdc05d16dd

+1 Master https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3426b751-7a40-4601-85c2-3ad280d899b9

(This sighting report is also updated under LU-18569)

client:  5.15.0-142-generic
Server:  4.18.0-553.53.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Client log (10.240.27.184)

[21791.916712] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21791.916741] nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding, still trying^M         
[21813.420585] LNet: 2 peer NIs in recovery (showing 2): 10.240.27.192@tcp, 10.240.27.186@tcp^M
[21813.422233] Lustre: 1054975:0:(client.c:2447:ptlrpc_expire_one_request()) @@@ Request sent has failed due to network error: [sent 1751553348/real 1751553536]  req@ffff9a538ab02700 x      1836635514129920/t0(0) o400->MGC10.240.27.192@tcp@10.240.27.192@tcp:26/25 lens 224/224 e 0 to 1 dl 1751553364 ref 1 fl Rpc:EeXNQU/200/ffffffff rc -5/-1 job:'kworker.0' uid:0 gid:0 proj      id:4294967295^M
[21813.428204] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.27.192@tcp at host 10.240.27.192:7988^M
[21814.444574] LNetError: Unexpected error -2 connecting to 10.240.27.192@tcp at host 10.240.27.192:7988^M

MDT

21638.716775] LNet: Timeout error while writing to 10.240.27.184:1021. Closing socket: rc = -110^M
[21701.597499] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000: haven't heard from client 3b2ebc9a-b2cf-458c-b92e-9388e6403003 (at 10.240.27.184@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9b887a3d2800, cur 1751553428 deadline 1751553427 last 1751553327^M
[21706.717635] Lustre: lustre-MDT0000: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 105 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9b886ef6a800, cur 1751553433 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M

OST

[21702.393354] Lustre: lustre-OST0005: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0c968ff000, cur 1751553429 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M
[21702.905333] Lustre: lustre-OST0006: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 101 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0c836ae800, cur 1751553429 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M
[21702.909148] Lustre: Skipped 1 previous similar message^M                     
[21704.953609] Lustre: lustre-OST0000: haven't heard from client cd1f08cf-b9d8-4971-997a-2d05d1ce6219 (at 10.240.27.185@tcp) in 103 seconds. I think it's dead, and I am evicting it. ex      p ffff9d0d83000000, cur 1751553431 deadline 1751553428 last 1751553328^M

This "nfs: server 10.240.16.204 not responding" issue caused 19 test session failures in the past week.

It does seem to hit more often in sanity-sec.sh (LU-18569) than other subtests (14x vs 5x) but it isn't in a consistent subtest in sanity-sec either, so it isn't clear that there is something in that test itself that is causing problems, unless the Kerberos or DNS server is running on the same node as the NFS server, since they may increase the load there?

LU-18569 is duplicating this issue and tracking it for sanity-sec. There may be several other duplicating issues, as this timeout can happen in any test. I've seen that issue since December, but it seems to be becoming worse (hard to say with the scattered issues). A common denominator seems to be that this network failure only occurs in the "review-ldiskfs-ubuntu" group.

Seemed to happen on sanity-sec.sh as well:

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/8baafccc-3e90-4a08-bcf5-1c22c1cea250

+6 on master in the past week (at a minimum, there are likely other failures not flagged):
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/1ef7009a-fd29-463c-9a2f-0af3d34d5931
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/d03f7e4b-a91a-428e-b7c4-49d8b0c4323b
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/c9009318-9e62-47dc-8180-c2f5f8130c27
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/ef03eba8-236c-4323-a5f9-9af5b33d0ba0
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/43b5c487-2fe1-451d-b47f-5cba51d552c6
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/d5bf95f6-c857-4269-9af4-e9c4c5d37683
